title: Chef

rationales.taskAutomation: Cooking, plating, and real-time kitchen judgment in an unstructured environment remain physical craft; only menu costing and reordering are cleanly automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI meal-kit and recipe-generation tools are widespread, but purpose-built kitchen robotics remain pilot-stage outside a handful of high-volume chains.
rationales.adoption: Independent restaurants and most chains still run kitchens on human judgment; AI shows up in back-office ordering and menu engineering, not on the line.
rationales.agenticExposure: Inventory reordering, prep-list generation, and food-cost tracking are agent-ownable back-office slices; live cooking is not.

tasksAtRisk: Basic recipe scaling for catering orders
tasksGrowing: Menu creativity and seasonal dish development
Live service judgment during a rush
Staff training and kitchen culture leadership
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-1011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Kitchen team leadership under live service pressure

insight: Line cooking and plating still require dexterity and split-second judgment no kitchen robot handles at scale, so chefs stay largely insulated. The exposure sits in the office: AI now drafts prep lists, forecasts food cost, and reorders inventory, shrinking the admin half of the job faster than the culinary half.
